note: Header row. Real records start after first REFRESH. Schema: id, source_ids, claim, observed_at (ISO8601), dossier, weight (0-1)

claim: LangChain 1.0 and LangGraph 1.0 GA released on Oct 22, 2025. No breaking changes committed until 2.0. 90M monthly downloads; used in production at Uber, LinkedIn, Klarna, Rippling, JP Morgan, Blackrock, Cisco.

create_agent is the new 1.0 standard entry point in LangChain, built on LangGraph runtime. AgentExecutor is deprecated; create_react_agent in langgraph.prebuilt is superseded. Migration is a few-line drop-in for simple ReAct loops, a rewrite into StateGraph for non-trivial cases.

Middleware is the new 1.0 customization surface in LangChain — before_agent / before_model / wrap_model_call / wrap_tool_call / after_model / after_agent hooks. Built-ins for Human-in-the-Loop, Summarization, PII redaction.
LangGraph 1.0 has durable state, built-in persistence, and first-class human-in-the-loop interrupts. The runtime primitive that justifies the framework for production long-running agents.
LangSmith Agent Builder renamed to LangSmith Fleet (Mar 2026). Adds agent identity, sharing, permissions. Polly GA. LangSmith Sandboxes Private Preview. Skills in LangSmith Fleet (Mar 2026).

deepagents v0.4 shipped Feb 17, 2026 with pluggable sandbox support, smarter conversation history summarization, Responses API as default for OpenAI. deepagents v0.5.0 alpha shipped April 2026 with async subagents, multi-modal support, Anthropic prompt caching improvements, ContextHubBackend.

Letta Code released Dec 16, 2025 — model-agnostic memory-first coding agent, the #1 OSS harness on Terminal-Bench. Achieves comparable performance to provider-specific harnesses (Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Codex CLI). Skills as .md files in git for versioning. Conversations API (Jan 21, 2026) for shared agent memory across concurrent experiences.

Letta released Context Constitution (Apr 2, 2026) — set of principles governing how AI agents manage context to learn from experience. Context Repositories (Feb 12, 2026) — git-based memory for coding agents. Skill Learning (Dec 2, 2025). Letta Code app (Apr 6, 2026) for local-machine use.

LangChain State of Agent Engineering 2026: 57% of organizations have agents in production. Quality is the top production killer (32%); cost concerns dropped year-over-year. 89% have observability for agents; 62% detailed tracing. 75%+ use multiple models — model diversity is the norm.

GPT-5.5 released April 23, 2026. Terminal-Bench 2.0: 82.7%. GDPval: 84.9%. SWE-Bench Pro: 58.6%. OSWorld-Verified: 78.7%. CyberGym: 81.8%. 1M context in Codex. API pricing: $5/M input, $30/M output. Treated as High under Preparedness Framework for biology and cybersecurity.

GPT-5.5 Instant became the new ChatGPT default on May 5, 2026, replacing GPT-5.3 Instant. OpenAI claims 52.5% reduction in hallucinated claims on high-stakes prompts (medicine, law, finance) versus GPT-5.3 Instant. Available in API as chat-latest.
OpenAI shipped new realtime voice models on May 7, 2026 — can reason, translate, and transcribe speech for more natural voice agents. Available in the realtime API, not in Codex CLI surface.

OpenAI frontier models and Codex generally available on AWS via Amazon Bedrock as of June 1, 2026 (Commercial + GovCloud). Daybreak (vision for secure software build) coming later. Customers announced: Amgen, Autodesk.

OpenAI introduced Rosalind Biodefense (May 29, 2026) and expanded trusted access to GPT-Rosalind for U.S. government and allied partners. Partners: Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, Johns Hopkins APL, CEPI. GPT-Rosalind released Apr 16, 2026 as frontier reasoning model for life sciences.

Anthropic Claude Opus 4.8 released May 28, 2026. Dynamic Workflows (research preview on Max/Team/Enterprise + API): Claude Code plans and runs hundreds of parallel subagents in a single session. Trigger keyword 'ultracode' (renamed from 'workflow'). Effort Control Low/Medium/High/Max. Fast Mode: up to 2.5x output tokens/sec at 3x lower cost. Mid-conversation System Messages preserve prompt cache hits.

Anthropic Managed Agents (Code w/ Claude SF May 12, 2026) shipped four features: Dreaming (scheduled process reviews past sessions, curates memory), Multiagent Orchestration (lead agent delegates to specialist subagents in parallel on shared filesystem, traceable in Claude Console), Outcomes (developer-defined rubric, separate grader context, +10pt task success on hardest problems), Webhooks. Customers: Spotify, Base44, Legora, Amplitude, Clay, Rogo.

Anthropic Managed Agents added Self-hosted Sandboxes (public beta, Cloudflare/Daytona/Modal/Vercel) and MCP tunnels (research preview) at Code w/ Claude London May 26, 2026. Self-hosted sandbox keeps files and repos in customer's perimeter. MCP tunnels let agents reach private-network MCP servers via a single outbound connection.

Google introduced Gemini 3.5 family at Google I/O 2026 (May 19, 2026). 3.5 Flash available same day in Gemini app, AI Mode in Google Search, AI Studio, Gemini Enterprise, Android Studio, Antigravity. 3.5 Pro in development, used internally, rolling out next month. Framed as 'frontier intelligence with action.'

Google introduced Gemini Omni at I/O 2026 — any-to-any model that takes any input (text, video, sound) and produces any output. Powers Google Pics, intelligent eyewear, Ask YouTube. Gemini Spark personal AI agent uses 3.5 Flash, runs 24/7, taking action on user's behalf.

Google Antigravity 2.0 launched at I/O 2026 — agent-first development platform for working with Gemini 3.5. Projects, scheduled tasks, subagents, slash commands. Compared to Claude Code and Codex CLI in 3rd-party benchmarking.

SubQ 1M-Preview from Subquadratic released May 5, 2026 — first commercial LLM with fully subquadratic sparse attention architecture, 12M token context window, ~1/5 cost of frontier models, claimed 52x faster attention at scale. Architecture could change long-context unit economics if benchmarks hold.

ZAYA1-8B from Zyphra (May 6-7, 2026) — Apache 2.0 MoE, 8B total params with ~760M active per token, trained end-to-end on AMD Instinct hardware. Available on Hugging Face and Zyphra Cloud serverless. Zyphra claims competitive performance vs much larger open-weight reasoning models.

Grok 4.3 (xAI) wider rollout May 6, 2026. Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite (Google) May 8, 2026 — lightweight Gemini 3.1 variant. Gemini 3.1 Pro (Feb 19, 2026) reported at 77.1% ARC-AGI-2 and 94.3% GPQA Diamond per third-party survey.

Hugging Face trending models May 2026: google/gemma-4-31B-it (8.9M dl), Qwen/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B (MoE, 3.6B active), deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro (1.3M dl), XiaomiMiMo/MiMo-V2.5-Pro (agent-focused, Chinese ecosystem), unsloth/MiniMax-M2.7-GGUF, openai/privacy-filter. Gemma 4 (Apr 2, 2026) open-weight for reasoning + agentic workflows.

GPT-5.6 appeared in Codex internal canary logs in mid-May 2026 — NO public release, no model card, no pricing sheet. Community prediction markets base case: GPT-5.6 Jun 2026, GPT-6 Q3-Q4 2026. Daybreak (OpenAI's vision for secure software build with cyber models + Codex Security) targeted for future AWS availability.

CONTRADICTED CLAIM: geo.fkw.com asserts OpenAI released GPT-6 on April 14, 2026 with 2M context, 5-6T params, Symphony multimodal architecture, 40% perf improvement over GPT-5.4. CONTRADICTED by OpenAI's own research index (src-002) which shows GPT-5.5 is the current frontier as of June 2, 2026. Single low-trust SEO source. Verification queue.

arXiv surveys (Du 2026: 2603.07670; Mnemonic Sovereignty 2604.16548) confirm agent memory is the active research frontier. Five mechanism families: context-resident compression, retrieval-augmented stores, reflective self-improvement, hierarchical virtual context, policy-learned management. MemoryArena benchmark: 40-60% on multi-session tasks vs near-perfect on LoCoMo — exposes deep gap between passive recall and active decision-relevant memory.

arXiv 2604.16548: persistent memory introduces novel security properties. InjecMEM (Tian 2026) achieves 61.4% retrieval success and 76.6% conditional attack success on MemoryOS/Qwen2.5-7B. eTAMP (Zou 2026): environmental-observation poisoning that activates across future sessions. SpAIware (Herrador & Rehberger 2026): indirect prompt injection plants persistent memory that activates in future sessions.

Production-tested framework ranking (Alice Labs + nxcode): LangGraph #1 for stateful workflows; Claude Agent SDK #2 (Anthropic-native, powers Claude Code); CrewAI #3 for role-based multi-agent crews; AutoGen/AG2 #4; Semantic Kernel #5 (.NET). CrewAI v1.10.1 (Mar 2026) 45.9K stars, native MCP + A2A, powers 12M+ daily agent executions.

Hermes Agent (Nous Research, MIT, Feb 2026) organizes around four properties: persistent memory (SQLite state.db with FTS5 + Honcho user modeling), skill system (SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter, skill_manage tool with create/patch/edit/delete, progressive disclosure, 30-day stale / 90-day archived lifecycle), scheduled automation (cron/jobs.json with chaining via context_from), MCP integration (Filesystem / Brave Search / GitHub / Puppeteer via .env). SOUL.md occupies slot #1 in the system prompt. Profiles are fully isolated Hermes instances with own config/memory/skills/sessions/SOUL.md.

Single-source observation: 'Most people who discover Hermes Agent spend the first week confused' — Hermes has a documented onboarding/UX gap. Self-reported by the masterclass author (CyrilXBT) as the motivation for writing the complete course. The gap is not a bug; it is the absence of a complete primary-source walkthrough from zero to operational. Corroborated by the proliferation of third-party tutorials (dailydoseofds, skool, CSDN, IT之家, jb51) all written in May-June 2026.

Researcher synthesized the Artemis Program mid-2026 status report covering Artemis II success (Apr 1, 2026), Artemis III restructuring (May 13, 2026, Earth-orbit docking test pushed to late 2027), lunar landing pushed to Artemis IV (2028), and HLS provider progress (SpaceX Starship test late May 2026, Blue Origin Blue Moon tracking 2027 deadline).
